goal:
Five looksmax decks that take a popular appearance goal and reframe it as a
health outcome ("the real fix is sleep, food, water, posture"). Each opens on
the aesthetic payoff + a contrarian reframe, delivers 5 honest levers, and ties
the exact levers to Vibe Health tracking. Round-two of the looksmax line — all
goals are distinct from the live decks (jawline, clear skin, under-eyes, hair,
leaner face, posture, beauty sleep, debloat, glow, tier list).

novelty: 39-whiter-smile: The gum-framing angle and enamel-thinning warning are fresher than the usual 'oil pull and eat strawberries' content, though water-rinsing after coffee is widely circulated.
40-fix-double-chin: Fluid-pooling and tech-neck angles are fresher than the usual 'do neck rolls' advice, though calorie deficit and sleep tips are well-trodden.
41-strong-nails: The biotin-debunk slide and iron-spoon callout are genuinely fresh angles most nail content skips; protein framing is less novel but still underused.
42-clear-body-skin: The sweat/friction framing is fresher than typical skincare carousels, though the individual tips (shower after gym, clean sheets) are familiar.
43-brighter-eyes: The individual tips (sleep, hydrate, blink rule) are well-known; the angle of framing it through eye-white clarity is a mild fresh spin but not a genuinely surprising reframe.
hook_strength: 39-whiter-smile: 'Starts with your gums' is a specific, contrarian reframe that subverts the obvious strip-bleaching assumption — solid scroll-stopper, but lacks a punchy number or concrete promise.
40-fix-double-chin: 'Rarely genetics' is a solid contrarian opener with a concrete 3-part diagnosis, though it lacks a sharp number or visual provocation to truly spike curiosity.
41-strong-nails: 'Nutrition readout' reframes nails as a body signal — specific and mildly contrarian, though not viscerally urgent enough to guarantee a stop.
42-clear-body-skin: 'It's not dirt' is a direct myth-bust that earns a second look, though it lacks a concrete outcome promise to fully seal the stop.
43-brighter-eyes: 'Bright white eyes read as healthy' is concrete and vanity-driven — stops scrollers who care about appearance, though it's not aggressively contrarian.

hooks: A whiter smile starts *with your gums*.
Clear up your back — *it's not dirt*.

summary: The batch landed strong on audience resonance and clarity — the health-reframe format is well-calibrated for the target audience and Vibe brand. Novelty (3.8) and hook strength (4.0) are the ceiling-limiters: angles are fresh but underlying tips are familiar, and hooks are contrarian without being urgent or specific enough to spike curiosity hard.
what_worked: Health-outcome reframe angle earns high audience_fit and brand_fit — keep it as the structural spine
Myth-bust hook structure ('not dirt', 'rarely genetics') earns second looks and fits the contrarian voice
Slide clarity and Vibe tracking tie-in are consistently well-executed
Biotin-debunk and gum-first framing are cited as genuinely fresh — mechanism-level novelty pays off when present
what_underperformed: Individual tips default to well-known advice (sleep, hydrate, deficit) even when the framing angle is fresh
Hooks lack a concrete number or measurable promise — contrarian reframes without specificity don't fully stop the scroll
Eye-whites deck barely reframed the mechanism; most tips were standard advice with a thin new label
No hook created visceral urgency or surprising visual provocation — all feel informational rather than alarming

strategies.title: Surface the non-obvious mechanism, not just a fresh angle
strategies.guidance: For each tip, ask: would a typical health creator say this? If yes, replace it. Target at least 3 of 5 tips per deck to be mechanism-level surprises (e.g. 'enamel thinning from whitening strips' not 'drink water after coffee'). The angle can be familiar if the underlying causal chain is not.

strategies.title: Anchor every hook with a concrete number or outcome promise
strategies.guidance: Contrarian reframes alone score ~4.0. Add a specific number, timeframe, or measurable claim to the hook slide to push toward 4.5+. Example pattern: '[contrarian reframe] — [X days / X% / X steps]'. The number creates urgency the reframe alone doesn't.

strategies.title: Make hooks feel like a warning, not just a correction
strategies.guidance: Hooks rated weakest when they were mildly surprising but not alarming. Rewrite hook candidates to carry mild threat or loss-framing ('you're making it worse', 'the thing most people do is backwards'). Informational hooks don't stop scrollers the way consequence hooks do.

strategies.title: Run a 'would a doctor say this on TikTok' filter before finalizing tips
strategies.guidance: Before locking a deck, review each tip: if it appears in the top 5 Google results for the topic, cut or deepen it. Each deck should contain at least one tip that addresses a less-obvious downstream cause (e.g. magnesium for jaw tension vs. 'reduce stress') to raise novelty without sacrificing credibility.
